input.background: Suppose we take an SRS of n=100 from a population in which 20% of individuals smoke.
input.question: Determine the distribution of the sample proportion of smokers.

output.method: 事件发生概率及独立性
output.answer: Sample proportion p̂ will vary according to a Normal distribution with μ=p and σ=√(pq/n)=√((0.20)(0.80)/100)=0.04; in other words, p̂ ∼ N(0.2,0.04).

n.description: Sample size, the number of individuals in the simple random sample (SRS).

sample_proportion.value: Not explicitly provided in the question, but it represents the proportion of smokers in the sample.
sample_proportion.description: The sample proportion of smokers, which is the variable of interest to determine its distribution.

meta_info.caption: Normal approximation to the binomial.
meta_info.section: 8.3 SAMPLING BEHAVIOR OF A COUNT AND PROPORTION
